title: Roman Anthony wears Charlie McAvoy’s USA Olympics jersey before WBC final
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony wore Charlie McAvoy's USA hockey jersey before the WBC final against Venezuela.
Team USA baseball members wore hockey jerseys from the 2026 Olympics at the World Baseball Classic final in Miami.
Roman Anthony, batting .318, has been a standout player for Team USA in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: Ex-Celtic Marcus Smart recalls 2018 incident that almost made him lose his right hand
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: In 2018, Marcus Smart punched a picture frame, resulting in a severe hand injury requiring 20 stitches.
Smart, now with the Lakers, still plays with glass in his hand, having avoided permanent damage to tendons.
Smart's post-Celtics career included stints with Memphis and Washington before joining the Lakers as a free agent.

title: Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Michael Chiang, 37, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his sister's recovery from a severe car accident.
Chiang's sister, Jennifer, relearned to walk and ran a 5K post-recovery after a significant surgical and rehabilitation process.
Michael Chiang, a former rehabilitation resident, experienced firsthand the impact of inpatient rehabilitation at Spaulding Hospital.

title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A body found in Lake Quannapowitt is believed to be missing Wakefield man Jason Thomas, 45, missing since December.
Preliminary investigation, including clothing, suggests the body is Thomas, with no foul play suspected, officials stated.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner will confirm the identity and cause of death of the recovered individual.

title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts bill to legalize online casino gambling sent to study, halting progress this legislative session.
The Joint Committee on Economic Development and Emerging Technologies voted 11-0 to study the bill, effectively shelving it.
Rep. David Muradian plans to refile the internet gaming bill for the 2027-2028 legislative session.

title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA bus crashed into cars and a Medford home on March 8, caused by drowsy or asleep driver.
The 40-year-old driver faces charges for negligent operation, failure to stop, and lane violations.
Investigators ruled out alcohol or intoxicating substances as factors in the early morning crash.

title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Black Mountain in Jackson, New Hampshire will not transition to a community co-op as initially planned.
General Manager Erik Mogensen purchased Black Mountain and plans to use it as a lab for solving independent ski area challenges.
Indy Pass and Entabeni Systems, led by Mogensen, will relocate their headquarters to Jackson, New Hampshire.

title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Julia Aldrich, a 35-year-old from Cincinnati, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Aldrich, a breast cancer survivor, was diagnosed at 32 and resumed racing just weeks after her double mastectomy.
This is Aldrich's fourth marathon and her first time running to raise funds for cancer research.

title: An expert explains why the recent Danvers murder stands out
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, allegedly killed 68-year-old Janet Swallows in a random home invasion in Danvers.
Criminologist James Alan Fox highlights the lack of clear motive in teenage murders like DeMayo's case.
DeMayo pleaded not guilty to murder charges and is undergoing evaluations at Bridgewater State Hospital.

title: MGB researchers just developed a new risk calculator for obesity. Here’s why that’s a game changer.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mass General Brigham researchers developed a genetic risk calculator to predict obesity-related health risks and long-term consequences.
The tool uses data from genome-wide studies spanning over 8.5 million participants globally, enhancing accuracy across diverse populations.
The calculator predicts the need for interventions like GLP-1 medications by identifying individuals with high genetic risk scores.
